title: Goals as Reference Points and Risk Taking
description: This study examines how goals influence decision-making and risk-taking. You will complete 3–6 short scenarios in which you either make choices (e.g., between a sure outcome vs. a risky option) or rate how acceptable another person's behavior is. Each scenario takes about 1–2 minutes. Total time: approximately 15–25 minutes.
consent_text: By participating in this study, you agree to: (1) Complete 3–6 short vignette scenarios about decision-making and judgments, (2) Allow your responses to be used for research purposes, (3) Understand that your responses will be de-identified for analysis. Your participation is voluntary and you may withdraw at any time. All data will be kept confidential and used only for research purposes. You may receive course credit via the PRAMS system. Contact Dr. Christopher Castille with questions.
